name: Arnold, Clara May
description: One who has covered herself with glory in her mental skirmishes.
text: One who has covered herself with glory in her mental skirmishes.

name: Autrey, John Lee
description: One who ever bore the grand old names—"Poke-easy" and "Freshman."
text: One who ever bore the grand old names—"Poke-easy" and "Freshman."

name: Barry, W. T.
description: Since taking to himself a wife, Mr. Barry has devoted himself assiduously to matrimony to the exclusion of all festivities.
text: Since taking to himself a wife, Mr. Barry has devoted himself assiduously to matrimony to the exclusion of all festivities.

name: Batten, John T.
description: An exceedingly popular young man.
text: An exceedingly popular young man.

name: Blair, Cecil Clyde
description: Since coming to the U. of A. he has impressed all who know him as a thorough student.
text: Since coming to the U. of A. he has impressed all who know him as a thorough student.

name: Bragg, Peter N.
description: All the girls say he has a romantic name.
text: All the girls say he has a romantic name.

name: Cammack, George S.
description: A courteous, good-natured man.
text: A courteous, good-natured man.

name: Cargile, L. Clare
description: Clare has the bearing of a senator and a military genius. Perhaps his success in love is in some way connected with his poise.
text: Clare has the bearing of a senator and a military genius. Perhaps his success in love is in some way connected with his poise.

name: Carl, Floyd C.
description: One more fitted to grapple with practical experiences would be hard to find.
text: One more fitted to grapple with practical experiences would be hard to find.

name: Cates, Allen W.
description: A man worth knowing. His dimples are truly wonderful, but besides he is of strength of character and ability.
text: A man worth knowing. His dimples are truly wonderful, but besides he is of strength of character and ability.

name: Davidson, Elmer Cruse
description: Elmer is known as a Chesterfield and a divine dancer. He is a man more wise than foolish. He works when you think he works not.
text: Elmer is known as a Chesterfield and a divine dancer. He is a man more wise than foolish. He works when you think he works not.

name: Decker, Kivikivia
description: We are appalled by the amount of Greek and Hebrew that Kivikivia knows.
text: We are appalled by the amount of Greek and Hebrew that Kivikivia knows.

name: Derden, J. H.
motto: "To know more about it."
description: A man well worth knowing. He makes school a business.
text:
"To know more about it."
A man well worth knowing. He makes school a business.

name: Dunn, John Howard
description: He used to be known as "Little Did," but now he's known by what he did. John is a very industrious chap.
text: He used to be known as "Little Did," but now he's known by what he did. John is a very industrious chap.

name: Forrest, Leland S.
description: Leland's business ability has been aptly demonstrated this year in his management of the Book Store.
text: Leland's business ability has been aptly demonstrated this year in his management of the Book Store.

name: Garrett, Claude W.
description: One who has loved many girls in his day.
text: One who has loved many girls in his day.

name: Gerig, Frank A.
description: He even has the grand old name of "Goat."
text: He even has the grand old name of "Goat."

name: Gilliam, Surrey E.
description: A man who does well at everything he attempts, as is evidenced by the many positions he has held.
text: A man who does well at everything he attempts, as is evidenced by the many positions he has held.

name: Harvey, Robin
description: Robin can do lots more than giggle. She can make seven E's at one time.
text: Robin can do lots more than giggle. She can make seven E's at one time.

name: Hughes, Anna Irene
description: Anna's eyes fairly dance with fun and even in the library when she is delving into knowledge she cannot always look serious.
text: Anna's eyes fairly dance with fun and even in the library when she is delving into knowledge she cannot always look serious.

name: Hughes, Jewell Constance
description: One who as a student has brought glory to herself and to her alma mater. She has the distinction of being one of the most brilliant math students who has ever gone out from this university.
text: One who as a student has brought glory to herself and to her alma mater. She has the distinction of being one of the most brilliant math students who has ever gone out from this university.

name: Izard, Letha
description: Letha is everyone's friend, the confidante of freshmen, the consoler of "fall guys" and "Shorty's" girl.
text: Letha is everyone's friend, the confidante of freshmen, the consoler of "fall guys" and "Shorty's" girl.

name: Joyner, J. Ed.
description: "Jed" preaches honesty and lives for the league. His greatest shortcoming is his excessive use of the telephone.
text: "Jed" preaches honesty and lives for the league. His greatest shortcoming is his excessive use of the telephone.

name: Kennard, Rolfe P.
description: His perpetual smile often belies his mood, for in spite of it he grinds—even in Spanish.
text: His perpetual smile often belies his mood, for in spite of it he grinds—even in Spanish.

name: Lee, Arthur W.
description: Arthur is a steadfast believer in the old adage, "Where there is a will, there's a way." He is also quite a physical culture enthusiast.
text: Arthur is a steadfast believer in the old adage, "Where there is a will, there's a way." He is also quite a physical culture enthusiast.

name: Lee, Robert D.
clubs: Inter-Collegiate Debater against University of Oklahoma, 1915
description: Robert is always a leader in debating and oratory.
text: Robert is always a leader in debating and oratory.

name: McCain, Dolph
description: One with whom we associate quiet dignity and refinement.
text: One with whom we associate quiet dignity and refinement.

name: McCulley, Icey May
description: Cool and collected at all times as shown by her name.
text: Cool and collected at all times as shown by her name.

name: Moore, Lyla
description: Lyla's sweet disposition is only surpassed by her beauty. She is a very interesting good girl to those who know her best.
text: Lyla's sweet disposition is only surpassed by her beauty. She is a very interesting good girl to those who know her best.

name: Newton, W. K.
description: One who puts heart, soul and enthusiasm into work—yet knocks a little sometimes.
text: One who puts heart, soul and enthusiasm into work—yet knocks a little sometimes.

name: O'Neal, Beatrice
description: Delicacy, refinement and tact make everyone love Beatrice.
text: Delicacy, refinement and tact make everyone love Beatrice.

name: Park, Mae
description: Although we know Mae devotes much of her time to study, she has time for an immense amount of fun and dates, too.
text: Although we know Mae devotes much of her time to study, she has time for an immense amount of fun and dates, too.

name: Parsons, Lloyd C.
description: "Shorty" is best described as a good fellow in its biggest, broadest sense.
text: "Shorty" is best described as a good fellow in its biggest, broadest sense.

name: Phillips, Bess
description: Bess has a manner which never fails to please.
text: Bess has a manner which never fails to please.

name: Porter, Florence Edwina
description: Known and liked by all. She has the widest toleration for each one's views and everyone's practices.
text: Known and liked by all. She has the widest toleration for each one's views and everyone's practices.

name: Price, Oscar G.
description: An ambitious piece of clay.
text: An ambitious piece of clay.

name: Robinson, Henry Evalyn
description: Evalyn never swerves from her duty, not even her duty as proctor.
text: Evalyn never swerves from her duty, not even her duty as proctor.

name: Rudolph, Freda
description: Freda is very obliging and good natured. Her sweet disposition has won her a host of friends.
text: Freda is very obliging and good natured. Her sweet disposition has won her a host of friends.

name: Southall, Richard C.
description: Never foredoomed to dejection or melancholy, Southall takes the world as he finds it, including the rope's end. A lawyer by inheritance.
text: Never foredoomed to dejection or melancholy, Southall takes the world as he finds it, including the rope's end. A lawyer by inheritance.

name: Smith, Euclid T.
description: A practical, straightforward, clean young American. Will publish a volume of poems soon.
text: A practical, straightforward, clean young American. Will publish a volume of poems soon.

name: Stewart, Reed
description: He stays away from University Hall. Doesn't he love the ladies? Reed has an unusual vocabulary for an engineer. He reads much. He delights in fine art. He is the greatest man from Three Creeks.
text: He stays away from University Hall. Doesn't he love the ladies? Reed has an unusual vocabulary for an engineer. He reads much. He delights in fine art. He is the greatest man from Three Creeks.

name: Swilley, George W.
description: George is a very devoted student. He thinks of his course and not of the ladies.
text: George is a very devoted student. He thinks of his course and not of the ladies.

name: White, T. T.
description: His song is a new version: "I love the books." Tell is young, but he is efficient.
text: His song is a new version: "I love the books." Tell is young, but he is efficient.

name: Willson, J. Freed
description: "Junie" has manifested his capability and proved the truth of his genius.
text: "Junie" has manifested his capability and proved the truth of his genius.

name: Redus, Frank B.
description: Well versed in all gentlemanly arts. A man with a great purpose. The Cardinal 1915
text: Well versed in all gentlemanly arts. A man with a great purpose. The Cardinal 1915

name: Harville, Archie W.
description: His laughter has cheered the heart of many a lady, but Arch expects to be a bachelor.
text: His laughter has cheered the heart of many a lady, but Arch expects to be a bachelor.

name: McCulloch, Richard B.
description: Even this mass of sternness is pierced by cupid's arrow.
text: Even this mass of sternness is pierced by cupid's arrow.

name: Dinwiddie, J. A.
clubs: Freshman, Sophomore and Junior Football Teams
description: He got his reputation as a full back and will maintain it in the engineering field.
text: He got his reputation as a full back and will maintain it in the engineering field.

name: Allen, Glen
motto: "Motley's the only wear."
text: "Motley's the only wear."

name: Armstrong, A. B.
motto: "Why does Abie do that?" "Do what?" "Oh, twist his pencil so funnily."
text: "Why does Abie do that?" "Do what?" "Oh, twist his pencil so funnily."

name: Barrow, Margaret
description: K. A. left out by request. Any information on K. A. given without charge.
text: K. A. left out by request. Any information on K. A. given without charge.

name: Arnold, Carrie
motto: "Here comes the lady, O so light afoot. Will ne'er wear out the everlasting flint."
text: "Here comes the lady, O so light afoot. Will ne'er wear out the everlasting flint."

name: Blanks, Lane
motto: "Oh, these are barren tasks, too hard to keep, Not to see ladies, study, fast, not sleep."
text: "Oh, these are barren tasks, too hard to keep, Not to see ladies, study, fast, not sleep."

name: Burnet, Jim
motto: "What's in a name? That which we call a rose By any other name would smell as sweet."
text: "What's in a name? That which we call a rose By any other name would smell as sweet."

name: Cochran, S. A.
motto: "What immortal hand or eye Framed thy fearful symmetry?"
text: "What immortal hand or eye Framed thy fearful symmetry?"

name: Costen, J. B.
motto: Chorus: "He's my Jim."
text: Chorus: "He's my Jim."

name: Clark, A. C.
motto: "A good portly man and corpulent; a cheerful look, a pleasing eye and most noble carriage."
text: "A good portly man and corpulent; a cheerful look, a pleasing eye and most noble carriage."

name: Coker, M. B.
motto: "There was a young man named Coker Who played in the band, Oh the joker. From out of his throat He blew such a note Through his sliding trombone that he broke her."
text: "There was a young man named Coker Who played in the band, Oh the joker. From out of his throat He blew such a note Through his sliding trombone that he broke her."

name: Carroll, J. C.
motto: "From his lips springs full-born divinest oratory."
text: "From his lips springs full-born divinest oratory."

name: Callahan, Margaret
motto: "Grace and good disposition attend your ladyship."
text: "Grace and good disposition attend your ladyship."

name: Curnutt, H. A.
motto: "A modest, sly, low-spoken man."
text: "A modest, sly, low-spoken man."

name: Carolan, Clem
motto: "Man is a noble animal."
text: "Man is a noble animal."

name: Carolan, T. L.
description: He thinks much, but says little.
text: He thinks much, but says little.

name: Chenault, Ella May
motto: "A smile is worth more than a grin in any market."
text: "A smile is worth more than a grin in any market."

name: Courson, W. H.
description: A man destined to play an important role in an economic way.
text: A man destined to play an important role in an economic way.

name: Dunn, Henry S.
description: The fellows call him "Speedy" and the girls wonder why.
text: The fellows call him "Speedy" and the girls wonder why.

name: Dubs, F. H.
motto: "Why man, he doth bestride the narrow world like a Colossus."
text: "Why man, he doth bestride the narrow world like a Colossus."

name: Ellington, F. M.
description: The man who caused the hair pulling in Carnall Hall by introducing election of the sponsors and maids.
text: The man who caused the hair pulling in Carnall Hall by introducing election of the sponsors and maids.

name: Frazier, E. H.
motto: "My appetite comes to me while eating."
text: "My appetite comes to me while eating."

name: Gray, Julius C.
motto: "Et tu, Brute."
description: Friend B.: "Egg for breakfast?"
text:
"Et tu, Brute."
Friend B.: "Egg for breakfast?"

name: Graves, Hester
motto: "Like glimpses of forgotten dreams."
text: "Like glimpses of forgotten dreams."

name: Geren, Jerry
motto: "A girl on the campus is worth two in the Hall."
description: He seldom ever surprises his professors with his knowledge.
text:
"A girl on the campus is worth two in the Hall."
He seldom ever surprises his professors with his knowledge.

name: Gill, Tapscott
description: "His pious air and saintly face Wears like a cloak with reverend grace."
text: "His pious air and saintly face Wears like a cloak with reverend grace."

name: Greer, I. M.
description: A studious, conscientious sort of a fellow.
text: A studious, conscientious sort of a fellow.

name: Goza, H. D.
description: "We grant, although he has much wit, He was very sly of using it."
text: "We grant, although he has much wit, He was very sly of using it."

name: Greaves, Clifton
description: His cogitative faculties immersed in the cogitruisidity of cogitation.
text: His cogitative faculties immersed in the cogitruisidity of cogitation.

name: Harris, Hadley
motto: "The fair, the chaste, the unexpressive she."
text: "The fair, the chaste, the unexpressive she."

name: Hinds, Helene
motto: "Her voice was ever soft, gentle and low—an excellent thing in woman."
text: "Her voice was ever soft, gentle and low—an excellent thing in woman."

name: Hilton, Esther
description: Noted for her common sense and tact.
text: Noted for her common sense and tact.

name: Holmes, O. G.
motto: "Deep on his front engraven Deliberation sat, and public care."
text: "Deep on his front engraven Deliberation sat, and public care."

name: Hunt, Ralph
motto: "I do know these—that therefore only are reputed wise for saying nothing."
text: "I do know these—that therefore only are reputed wise for saying nothing."

name: Hazlewood, W. S.
motto: "Company, villainous company, has been the spoil of me."
text: "Company, villainous company, has been the spoil of me."

name: Harville, W. E.
motto: "Though short my stature, yet my name extends To heaven itself and earth's remotest ends."
text: "Though short my stature, yet my name extends To heaven itself and earth's remotest ends."

name: Hooper, O. C.
motto: "Why look you so stern and tragic?"
text: "Why look you so stern and tragic?"

name: Hicks, Homer
motto: "Seldom he smiles, and smiles in such a sort As though he scorned the spirit that could laugh at anything."
text: "Seldom he smiles, and smiles in such a sort As though he scorned the spirit that could laugh at anything."

name: Henry, J. D.
description: But still his tongue ran on, the less of weight it bore, with greater ease.
text: But still his tongue ran on, the less of weight it bore, with greater ease.

name: Horton, W. G.
motto: "Oh, that this too, too solid flesh would melt."
text: "Oh, that this too, too solid flesh would melt."

name: Horton, H. R.
motto: "They grew in beauty side by side."
text: "They grew in beauty side by side."

name: Higgs, M. T.
motto: "Most honored royal highness, High Mogul of Gray Hall, I salute you."
text: "Most honored royal highness, High Mogul of Gray Hall, I salute you."

name: Irby, N. M.
motto: "I may justly say with the hook-nosed fellow of Rome, 'I came, I saw, I conquered.'"
text: "I may justly say with the hook-nosed fellow of Rome, 'I came, I saw, I conquered.'"

name: Knoch, Elmo
motto: "Give thy thoughts no tongue."
text: "Give thy thoughts no tongue."

name: Lano, Mildred
motto: "She is so good she would pour rose water on a toad."
text: "She is so good she would pour rose water on a toad."

name: Lincoln, Blanche
motto: "She dwelt in the untrodden paths, far from the haunts of man."
text: "She dwelt in the untrodden paths, far from the haunts of man."

name: Lee, W. D.
motto: "He hath eaten me out of house and home."
text: "He hath eaten me out of house and home."

name: Lincoln, Adeline
motto:
"O thou by nature taught
To breathe her genuine thought
In numbers warmly pure and sweetly strong."
text:
"O thou by nature taught
To breathe her genuine thought
In numbers warmly pure and sweetly strong."

name: Matthews, Jim P.
motto: "Indispensable to Y. W. C. A., Junior Class and Carnall Hall."
text: "Indispensable to Y. W. C. A., Junior Class and Carnall Hall."

name: Middlebrooks, Edna
motto: "She has delved into the depths of Rome and is a master of classical lore."
text: "She has delved into the depths of Rome and is a master of classical lore."

name: Moore, Lucille
motto: "She has been at the feast of languages and has stolen the scraps."
text: "She has been at the feast of languages and has stolen the scraps."

name: Moore, Vaughan
motto: "He keeps the noiseless tenor of his way."
text: "He keeps the noiseless tenor of his way."

name: Mackey, Minnie
motto: "Faithful unto the end."
text: "Faithful unto the end."

name: Matthews, B. B.
description: He has occasional flashes of silence that make his conversation perfectly delightful.
text: He has occasional flashes of silence that make his conversation perfectly delightful.

name: Mather, Juliette
description: A bright gentle thing, like the dawn of the morn or the dew of the spring.
text: A bright gentle thing, like the dawn of the morn or the dew of the spring.

name: Murrey, Joe
motto: "Who broke no promise, served no private end, Who gained no title and who lost no friend."
text: "Who broke no promise, served no private end, Who gained no title and who lost no friend."

name: McConnell, W. W.
motto: "Honest labor bears a lovely face."
text: "Honest labor bears a lovely face."

name: Nunn, Henry E.
motto: "None but himself can be his parallel."
text: "None but himself can be his parallel."

name: Mcbride, Edgar
motto: "Lord, I wonder what fool it was that first invented kissing."
text: "Lord, I wonder what fool it was that first invented kissing."

name: Mcdonald, Louise
motto: "Let the world slide."
text: "Let the world slide."

name: Oliver, J. W.
motto: "When every blade of grass on the campus shall be arranged in order."
description: A second Carlyle.
text:
"When every blade of grass on the campus shall be arranged in order."
A second Carlyle.

name: Osborne, Virginia
description: Enough, and over measure.
text: Enough, and over measure.

name: Oneal, Lloyd E.
description: The Edison of the dormitories.
text: The Edison of the dormitories.

name: Rudd, James T.
motto: "I never felt the kiss of love, Nor maiden's hand in mine."
text: "I never felt the kiss of love, Nor maiden's hand in mine."

name: Rice, Philip
motto: "I am not in the role of common men."
text: "I am not in the role of common men."

name: Riddling, Little
motto: "This fellow picks up wit, as pigeons peas, And utters it again when Jove doth please."
text: "This fellow picks up wit, as pigeons peas, And utters it again when Jove doth please."

name: Rawlings, T. P.
description: Idleness is an appendix to nobility.
text: Idleness is an appendix to nobility.

name: Snyder, Bryan
motto: "Hang sorrow! Care will kill a cat."
text: "Hang sorrow! Care will kill a cat."

name: Smith, Harold
motto: "How happy the life unembarrassed by the cares of business."
text: "How happy the life unembarrassed by the cares of business."

name: Stewart, C. J.
motto: "Who to himself is law no law doth need, Offends no law, and is a king indeed."
text: "Who to himself is law no law doth need, Offends no law, and is a king indeed."

name: Simco, Allie
motto: "A thing of beauty is a joy forever."
text: "A thing of beauty is a joy forever."

name: Smith, F. B.
motto: "I am resolved to grow fat and look young till forty."
text: "I am resolved to grow fat and look young till forty."

name: Sadler, W. P.
motto: "A lion among ladies is a most dreadful thing."
text: "A lion among ladies is a most dreadful thing."

name: Stewart, Jessie
motto: "Her greatest oath is but by seynt Loy."
text: "Her greatest oath is but by seynt Loy."

name: Smith, O. D.
motto: "I have touched the highest point of all my greatness."
text: "I have touched the highest point of all my greatness."

name: Tanner, Joe L.
motto:
"The music that from the soul doth rise
Doth make us dance divine."
text:
"The music that from the soul doth rise
Doth make us dance divine."

name: Rogers, Clementine
motto: "As smooth as monumental alabaster."
text: "As smooth as monumental alabaster."

name: Thomas, Alvin
motto: "Onward Christian soldiers."
text: "Onward Christian soldiers."

name: Tipton, Goodwin
Taylor, Irene
description: Her expression is most perfect; she looks right through the hearts of men. "Dramatics" is her middle name.
text: Her expression is most perfect; she looks right through the hearts of men. "Dramatics" is her middle name.

name: Winn, James
motto: "Lend me the flourish of all gentle tongues, Fie, painted rhetoric."
text: "Lend me the flourish of all gentle tongues, Fie, painted rhetoric."

name: White, Eddie
motto: "There are daggers in men's smiles."
text: "There are daggers in men's smiles."

name: Wilkes, J. C.
motto: "I was never less alone than when by myself."
text: "I was never less alone than when by myself."

name: Wofford, C. A.
description: Originator of the story of the stick book in the well.
text: Originator of the story of the stick book in the well.

name: Wells, G. C.
motto: "Why so dull and mute, young man, Prithee why so mute?"
text: "Why so dull and mute, young man, Prithee why so mute?"

name: Woodfin, Eugene
motto: "There is no credit in being jolly."
text: "There is no credit in being jolly."

name: Webster, Ima
motto: "A face of Grecian mold."
text: "A face of Grecian mold."

name: Woods, L. P.
motto: "Whose honors with increase of ages grow As streams roll down enlarging as they go."
text: "Whose honors with increase of ages grow As streams roll down enlarging as they go."

name: Watson, Lois
description: How quietly and softly she passes!
text: How quietly and softly she passes!

name: Wooten, W. R.
motto: "The loud laugh that bespeaks the vacant mind."
text: "The loud laugh that bespeaks the vacant mind."

name: Woody, Sue
description: That sweet child. She's a dear.
text: That sweet child. She's a dear.

name: Wilson, Donald D.
description: Encyclopædia Arkansata.
text: Encyclopædia Arkansata.

name: Oster, Mabel
description: Is fond of Monté Né picnics.*
text: Is fond of Monté Né picnics.*

name: Brown, Robert
description: Dr. Thomas' history shark. His side-line is capturing freshman strongholds.
text: Dr. Thomas' history shark. His side-line is capturing freshman strongholds.

name: Martin, Ray
description: An agri by profession, a soldier by request, and a politician by nature.
text: An agri by profession, a soldier by request, and a politician by nature.

name: Cabeen, Catherine
motto: "Nature made her what she is, And ne'er made such another."
text: "Nature made her what she is, And ne'er made such another."

name: Brennan, Dorothy
motto: "The vanilla of society."
text: "The vanilla of society."

name: Coffey, Jewell
motto: "O Romeo, Romeo, wherefore art thou Romeo?"
text: "O Romeo, Romeo, wherefore art thou Romeo?"

name: Decker, Klerchia
motto: "She would not thoughtlessly put her mind on a beau."
text: "She would not thoughtlessly put her mind on a beau."

name: Eld, Ellen
motto: "Women of few words are the best women."
text: "Women of few words are the best women."

name: Green, Una
motto: "As chaste as unsunned snow."
text: "As chaste as unsunned snow."

name: Chester, Allbright
motto: "A word to the wise is useless."
text: "A word to the wise is useless."

name: Austin, R. H.
motto: "A courteous gentleman, one in short distinctly worth while and a likeable sort."
text: "A courteous gentleman, one in short distinctly worth while and a likeable sort."

name: Allis, D. M.
motto: "That mysterious guy."
text: "That mysterious guy."

name: Blanks, A. G.
motto: "The youngest, but by no means the baby of the class."
text: "The youngest, but by no means the baby of the class."

name: Best, J. B.
motto: "The 'best' in the class."
text: "The 'best' in the class."

name: Bradley, Burnelle
motto: "She that hath a merry heart, hath a continual feast."
text: "She that hath a merry heart, hath a continual feast."

name: Brown, Kathleen
motto: "Distinguished by good nature and common sense."
text: "Distinguished by good nature and common sense."

name: Brown, Sarah Hazel
motto: "Girls, beware. I can beat anybody's time."
text: "Girls, beware. I can beat anybody's time."

name: Burr, E. E.
motto: "The ladyfusser."
text: "The ladyfusser."

name: Brooke, Paul
motto: "Show me, I'm from Missouri."
text: "Show me, I'm from Missouri."

name: Carter, J. I.
motto: "Nothing ever, hardly ever troubles me."
text: "Nothing ever, hardly ever troubles me."

name: Clark, C. L.
motto: "Cecil is the ladies' man of the Sophomore class."
text: "Cecil is the ladies' man of the Sophomore class."

name: Cannon, A. R.
motto: "There's many a slip 'twixt a Freshman and his dip."
text: "There's many a slip 'twixt a Freshman and his dip."

name: Krone, Marie
motto: "Golden opinions she hath from all sorts of people."
text: "Golden opinions she hath from all sorts of people."

name: Calhoun, Irene
motto: "She is all that fancy can paint her, she is lovely, she is divine."
text: "She is all that fancy can paint her, she is lovely, she is divine."

name: Hamilton, S. D.
motto: "A very impressive youth."
text: "A very impressive youth."

name: Cabe, Ethel
motto: "Study is a dreary thing; I would I knew the remedy."
text: "Study is a dreary thing; I would I knew the remedy."

name: Clark, Eula
motto: "A little while to suffer and to wait."
text: "A little while to suffer and to wait."

name: Carmichael, Lentes
motto: "The pride of the Sophomore class."
text: "The pride of the Sophomore class."

name: Critz, Eileen
motto: "She is so charitable and so pious."
text: "She is so charitable and so pious."

name: Craig, A. H.
motto: "Scoop, the cub reporter."
text: "Scoop, the cub reporter."

name: Duncan, Irene
motto: "Music hath power to soothe the savage breast."
text: "Music hath power to soothe the savage breast."

name: Cook, Jake
motto: "The athlete of the class. Has never been caught at class."
text: "The athlete of the class. Has never been caught at class."

name: Dodd, G. K.
motto: "There is a necessary limit to our achievements, but he had never reached it."
text: "There is a necessary limit to our achievements, but he had never reached it."

name: Flora, B. C.
motto: "If I can't sleep nights, I can sleep in class."
text: "If I can't sleep nights, I can sleep in class."

name: Ford, C. B.
motto: "Unobtrusive and quiet, as shy as a maid is desired to be, and of girls made afraid."
text: "Unobtrusive and quiet, as shy as a maid is desired to be, and of girls made afraid."

name: Fisher, Merlin
motto: "Ready and willing and most capable too. Always on hand his part to do."
text: "Ready and willing and most capable too. Always on hand his part to do."

name: Greenhaw, F. J.
motto: "Wins debates without half trying."
text: "Wins debates without half trying."

name: Heath, I. J.
motto: "A genial disposition wins its owner many friends."
text: "A genial disposition wins its owner many friends."

name: Hendrix, Carey
motto: "He is almost city broke, but still a little shy."
text: "He is almost city broke, but still a little shy."

name: Horner, Zena
motto: "Greater people than I have lived, but I don't believe it."
text: "Greater people than I have lived, but I don't believe it."

name: Huston, Mary
motto: "If Mary were in danger, would Press Warner?"
text: "If Mary were in danger, would Press Warner?"

name: Hill, Fannie May
motto: "I care for nobody, no not I, if nobody cares for me."
text: "I care for nobody, no not I, if nobody cares for me."

name: Jones, D. W.
motto: "After I get married, I can look around for a place to farm."
text: "After I get married, I can look around for a place to farm."

name: Harrington, Alice
motto: "It is prophesied that Alice will be a Perky-later (Percolator)."
text: "It is prophesied that Alice will be a Perky-later (Percolator)."

name: Jones, Annie Laurie
motto: "Her conscience is the fear of being found out."
text: "Her conscience is the fear of being found out."

name: Hill, Sue
motto: "I want to be loved like a leading lady in a regular Broadway play."
text: "I want to be loved like a leading lady in a regular Broadway play."

name: Jobe, Dalton
motto: "Would that he might see himself as others see him."
text: "Would that he might see himself as others see him."

name: Harrell, Tracy
motto: "If a look judicial and wise helps a lawyer, here’s one that will rise."
text: "If a look judicial and wise helps a lawyer, here’s one that will rise."

name: Kuykendall, Sam
motto: "Kept from a football 'A' only by reasons of state."
text: "Kept from a football 'A' only by reasons of state."

name: Heerwagen, P. K.
motto: "If he is as good as he looks he is all right."
text: "If he is as good as he looks he is all right."

name: Lawson, H. M.
motto: "Little, but loud."
text: "Little, but loud."

name: Kindley, Ola
motto: "Love maketh a woman a sensitive thing."
text: "Love maketh a woman a sensitive thing."

name: Langford, Harold
motto: "Speaks little and to the purpose."
text: "Speaks little and to the purpose."

name: Johnson, Scott
motto: "Pride of the ladies."
text: "Pride of the ladies."

name: Lanford, Nelle
motto: "When as a child, I spake as a child, but now that I am a woman, I retain my childish ways."
text: "When as a child, I spake as a child, but now that I am a woman, I retain my childish ways."

name: Johnson, B. E.
motto: "My thought is easy, but my expression hard."
text: "My thought is easy, but my expression hard."

name: Morton, Ruth
motto: "I am wrapt in dismal thinking."
text: "I am wrapt in dismal thinking."

name: Myers, Carlton B.
motto: "Let's elect Myers. What office is it? He is all right."
text: "Let's elect Myers. What office is it? He is all right."

name: McCoy, Nora
motto: "God hangs the greatest weights upon the smallest wires."
text: "God hangs the greatest weights upon the smallest wires."

name: McNair, Effie

text: "Love is the greatest of educators."

name: Merrill, W. D.
motto: "Not a brother to 'Frank.'"
text: "Not a brother to 'Frank.'"

name: McDonald, G. W.
motto: "Wanted—Some nerve; apply to McDonald and Palmer."
text: "Wanted—Some nerve; apply to McDonald and Palmer."

name: Nichols, Gelene
motto: "Knowledge is power."
text: "Knowledge is power."

name: Newman, Herbert
motto: "He never hurries, never worries, and never talks too much."
text: "He never hurries, never worries, and never talks too much."

name: Oates, F. B.
motto: "The pet of the dormitory council."
text: "The pet of the dormitory council."

name: Prothro, R. E.
motto: "Is Welsh, not Italian."
text: "Is Welsh, not Italian."

name: Pendleton, Myrtle
motto: "Her dignity and refinement account in a measure for her popularity."
text: "Her dignity and refinement account in a measure for her popularity."

name: Polk, Linda
motto: "The shortest way to a man's heart is through his stomach."
text: "The shortest way to a man's heart is through his stomach."

name: Poyner, P. N.
motto: "Everybody's friend and nobody's enemy."
text: "Everybody's friend and nobody's enemy."

name: Pape, Frank D.
motto: "He gambled on his beauty and thereby lost a dollar."
text: "He gambled on his beauty and thereby lost a dollar."

name: Randolph, J. R.
motto: "The boy with the Hot Springs laugh and Cuba-nola smile."
text: "The boy with the Hot Springs laugh and Cuba-nola smile."

name: Stanley, M. W.
motto: "I am an extraordinary man."
text: "I am an extraordinary man."

name: Stevenson, J. E.
motto: "Some agricultural..."
text: "Some agricultural..."

name: Thompson, Joe
motto: "A contented spirit is the sweet of existence."
text: "A contented spirit is the sweet of existence."

name: Thompson, O. C.
motto: "Speak fitly or be silently wise."
text: "Speak fitly or be silently wise."

name: Stephenson, Ola
motto: "Who chooseth me, shall choose what many men desire."
text: "Who chooseth me, shall choose what many men desire."

name: Thomas, C. O.
motto: "Seldom seen, never noticed."
text: "Seldom seen, never noticed."

name: Smith, Myrtle
motto: "The soul is dead that slumbers, And things are not what they seem."
text: "The soul is dead that slumbers, And things are not what they seem."

name: Scurlock, Stella
motto: "Had I been present at the creation I would have given some useful hints for the better ordering of the universe."
text: "Had I been present at the creation I would have given some useful hints for the better ordering of the universe."

name: Stewart, Clyde
motto: "Nothing is impossible to a willing mind."
text: "Nothing is impossible to a willing mind."

name: Sailor, V. L.
motto: "He was a parfait, gentil knight."
text: "He was a parfait, gentil knight."

name: Wakefield, G. E.
motto: "I'll find a way or make one."
text: "I'll find a way or make one."

name: Uzzelle, Jack
motto: "He is almost tamed."
text: "He is almost tamed."

name: Taylor, C. E.
motto: "Pet of the French I class."
text: "Pet of the French I class."

name: Trimble, J. W.
motto: "President—'nuff said. Let no dog bark."
text: "President—'nuff said. Let no dog bark."

name: Wozencraft, Anna
motto: "'Nuff said."
text: "'Nuff said."

motto: "A Freshman kidnapper. She talketh much."
text: "A Freshman kidnapper. She talketh much."

name: Cochran, Maurice
motto: "That handsome Mr. Cochran," the ladies say.
text: "That handsome Mr. Cochran," the ladies say.

name: Templeton, Jim
motto: Diligence is the mother of good luck.
text: Diligence is the mother of good luck.

motto: A little learning is a dangerous thing—that's why I never get even a little.
text: A little learning is a dangerous thing—that's why I never get even a little.

name: Boyd, D. T.
motto: Thinks a great deal, but never says anything about it.
text: Thinks a great deal, but never says anything about it.

motto: A live wire in the Political Equality League. Has great ambitions.
text: A live wire in the Political Equality League. Has great ambitions.

name: McClurkin, Daisy
motto: My thoughts are heard in heaven.
text: My thoughts are heard in heaven.

name: Wilson, A. L.
motto: Noted for his gastronomic and checker-playing feats.
text: Noted for his gastronomic and checker-playing feats.

motto: He never does today what he can put off till tomorrow.
text: He never does today what he can put off till tomorrow.

motto: She seldom smiles.
text: She seldom smiles.

name: Williams, Edna
motto: Eddie is not as shy as she looks.
text: Eddie is not as shy as she looks.

name: Walls, J. A.
motto: Is exceedingly clever with his hands.
text: Is exceedingly clever with his hands.

motto: The jaw-bone of the Sophomore class.
text: The jaw-bone of the Sophomore class.

name: Shelton, Effie
motto: She possesses the art of leadership.

motto: "Consistency, thou art a jewel."
text: "Consistency, thou art a jewel."

motto: "Did Vee get proctored?"
text: "Did Vee get proctored?"
